steps.n: Added مْ to make it negative. This changes it from meaning 'surely' to meaning 'not' when talking about past actions.

Added أَ at the beginning to turn this into a question. This combination (أَلَمْ) is commonly used to ask 'Did you not...?' when talking about past actions. Here it's asking 'Did you not see?' about what Allah did.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at the beginning to mean 'you' and changed the word pattern to make it present tense (happening now) instead of past tense (already happened)
The hamza and its vowel change to ى because it's easier to pronounce

steps.m: you see (in أَلَمْ تَرَ meaning 'did you not see/consider')
steps.n: The ى is dropped because it comes after أَلَمْ (did not) which requires this form. Together أَلَمْ تَرَ means 'did you not see/consider'
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable - fatha on ك and sukoon on ي
Adding fatha at the end to complete the word
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word tells what 'your Lord' (رَبُّكَ) did.
Root ر-ب-ب became رَبُّ (Lord) - the one who nurtures and takes care of others
The كَ at the end means 'your'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb فَعَلَ (did/acted).
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the word to show someone who does the action
Changed from single companion to plural companions using the pattern أَفْعَال (like changing 'friend' to 'friends')

steps.m: with/by the companions (of ٱلْفِيلِ - the elephant)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'with/by' and ِ at end to show connection to the word after it (the elephant)
Added kasra to make the basic noun form for 'elephant'
Added ال to make it definite ('the') and kasra at the end because it follows بِ (with/by) in بِأَصْحٰبِ (with/by the companions of)
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
Added مْ to turn 'did' into 'did not' - this makes it a question expecting 'yes' as the answer. Works with يَجْعَلْ (make) to ask 'Did He not make?'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

The يَ at the start shows it's happening now. The sukun marks (ْ) are added because this word comes after أَلَمْ (did not), which requires these marks. Together أَلَمْ يَجْعَلْ means 'did he not make?'

steps.m: to plot secretly with bad intentions
steps.n: This describes making secret plans, usually with bad intentions
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'a plot'. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَجْعَلْ (make/render) in this ayah.
Added هُمْ at the end to show the plot belongs to them. The َ (fatha) before هُمْ is needed to connect it properly.

steps.m: in (connects تَضْلِيلٍ to the verb يَجْعَلْ)
steps.n: This is a basic word that shows something is located inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects 'make' (يَجْعَلْ) with 'astray' (تَضْلِيلٍ) to show their plan was made into nothing.

steps.m: in (connects تَضْلِيلٍ to the verb يَجْعَلْ)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under ف shows this word is connecting something to what comes after it

steps.m: to lose the way, to wander off the right path
steps.n: This describes the action of getting lost or going off the right path

steps.m: leading astray, causing to go off the right path
steps.n: Changes from just 'being lost' to 'making someone else lost'
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this word is connected to the word فِى (in) that came before it in the ayah. Together they mean 'in leading astray'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to be sent (subject is Allah)
steps.n: Adding hamza makes the verb causative. The sukun (ْ) is added because when أ is added at the start, the first root letter gets a sukun.

steps.m: and he caused to be sent (subject is Allah)
steps.n: Adding و at the start connects this action to what came before it in the story about the People of the Elephant

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something happening on top of or against وَأَرْسَلَ)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something happening on top of or against something else. Here it connects with the verb 'sent' (وَأَرْسَلَ) to show what was sent upon.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the next part
Adding هِمْ shows this affects multiple people (them). In this ayah, it refers to the people of the elephant who were attacked.

steps.m: to fly or soar through the air

steps.n: Changed from action to noun by adding vowel marks. This word is what was sent (object) in the phrase 'He sent upon them birds.'
Added ًا to show this is the object receiving the action of أَرْسَلَ (He sent). The tanween (ً) makes it indefinite, meaning any birds.
Creates a special plural form showing many groups coming one after another. In this ayah, it describes how the birds came in continuous waves or groups to attack.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing the birds (طَيْرًا) mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fathas to show past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
The ت at start shows 'she' and the word pattern shows it's happening now (present tense), changed from 'he threw' in the past

steps.m: she throws at them (with بِحِجَارَةٍ stones)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show who is being thrown at. This connects to the next word بِحِجَارَةٍ which tells us she throws stones at them.

steps.m: to block or prevent with something solid

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and create the basic noun for a single stone
Changed to plural form by adding alif and changing first vowel to kasra
Added ة to make it a collective noun referring to stones as a group
Added بِ at beginning to show these stones are being used as tools (to strike with)

steps.m: with stones (object of تَرْمِيهِم - strike them)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object being used to strike them in the phrase 'strike them with stones'
This is a preposition showing that the stones are made from sijjeel (baked clay)
The م gets a shadda (doubled) because it comes after a word ending with a vowel (بِحِجَارَةٍ). This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to kasra and added shadda and yaa to show intensified quality. The meaning shifted from recording to the hard material that holds records/inscriptions.
Added tanwin kasra to show this describes the type of stones (حِجَارَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is about something that happened in the past

steps.m: and so he made (them like كَعَصْفٍ مَّأْكُولٍۭ - eaten-up chaff)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this was Allah's response to what they did. This connects to the next words that describe what He made them into.

steps.m: and so He made them (like كَعَصْفٍ مَّأْكُولٍۭ - eaten-up chaff)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was transformed - them (referring to أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْفِيلِ - the companions of the elephant)

steps.m: to blow away or scatter violently

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun showing the action itself
Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison and tanwin kasra at the end because it's being compared to the word مَّأْكُولٍۭ (eaten up) that comes after it
Changed from active to passive - shows what was eaten rather than who did the eating. This word describes the state of عَصْف (chaff/straw) in the ayah.
Added tanween kasra to show this describes what something is like, matching كَعَصْفٍ (like chaff/straw) before it
Added shadda on م to emphasize the complete consumption, showing how thoroughly destroyed they were
